sections.one_line: Why agent memory needs separate stores for narrative and durable facts
How Kernos keeps conversational history and durable facts in separate stores
Single-call fact harvesting that reconciles new, updated, and reinforced facts against the current store
Store refresh happens at compaction or space-switch boundaries, not every turn
Per-member separation of ledgers and facts in multi-member installs
How the two-store model fixes summary drift, duplicate facts, contradictions, and lost truths
Capabilities this architecture enables for long-term grounding, stable facts, updates, and privacy

sections.section_title: What this architecture explicitly does not try to do
sections.one_line: Architecture non-goals: what Kernos does not model, refresh, or extract from history
Related architecture topics for context spaces, disclosure layering, and cohort design
Key code entry points for fact harvesting, compaction, and member-scoped state
